edges.description: SmolLM3-3B-Base uses intra-document attention masking similar to Llama 3 to enable faster and more stable long-context training; the README cites Llama 3 as the methodological source.

anchor_list.excerpt: **Intra-Document Masking:** During training, we use attention masking to ensure tokens from different documents in the same training sequence don't attend to each other. Similar to Llama 3, this helps with faster and more stable long context training
anchor_list.explanation: Direct attribution to Llama 3 as the source of the intra-document masking strategy used in SmolLM3 pretraining.

anchor_list.excerpt: Similar to Llama 3, this helps with faster and more stable long context training while maintaining short context performance. ... we used LLama 3.2 tokenizer as is
anchor_list.explanation: Documents intra-document masking borrowed from Llama 3 and tokenizer reuse from Llama 3.2 (a Llama 3 family release).
anchor_list.position: Pretraining > Architecture and training details / Community comments

anchor_list.excerpt: Similar to Llama 3, this helps with faster and more stable long context training while maintaining short context performance.
anchor_list.explanation: Source attributes the intra-document masking choice to a recipe popularized by Llama 3.
anchor_list.position: Architecture and training details > Intra-Document Masking paragraph

description_variants: SmolLM3-3B-Base uses intra-document attention masking similar to Llama 3 to enable faster and more stable long-context training; the README cites Llama 3 as the methodological source.

SmolLM3 borrows intra-document attention masking from Llama 3 (tokens from different documents in the same sequence don't attend) and reuses the Llama 3 tokenizer; the comments confirm the team used the Llama 3.2 tokenizer as-is.

SmolLM3 adopts intra-document attention masking 'similar to Llama 3' to ensure tokens from different documents in the same sequence don't attend to each other, helping with faster and more stable long context training.

description_variants: Llama-3-70B-Instruct produced the educational-value annotation labels (~450k samples per HF model card) used as training targets for the FineWeb-Edu Classifier regression head.

Training labels (educational-quality scores 0-5) for the classifier were generated by Meta-Llama-3-70B-Instruct annotating FineWeb samples; the resulting HuggingFaceFW/fineweb-edu-llama3-annotations dataset is the classifier's training set.

The educational-quality scores (0-5) that serve as the regression targets for classifier training are synthetic labels generated by Llama-3-70B-Instruct prompted on the first 1000 characters of each FineWeb web sample; the classifier therefore inherits Llama-3-70B-Instruct's notion of educational quality.

The educational-value scores in HuggingFaceFW/fineweb-edu-llama3-annotations (the classifier's training labels) were annotated by Llama-3 (the README marks the dataset as 'Llama3-annotated educational value dataset'); the classifier therefore distills Llama-3-70B-Instruct's educational-quality judgments.
Llama-3-70B-Instruct generated the educational-quality scores (0-5) that supervise the classifier; the Yuan et al. additive prompt grades grade-school/middle-school knowledge.

The 0–5 educational scores that became the classifier's training labels were generated by Llama-3-70B-Instruct using the additive 5-point prompt (Yuan et al. self-rewarding scheme adapted to educational scoring; prompt in Appendix F.1).

Llama-3-70B-Instruct was prompted with the first 1000 characters of each FineWeb web page to produce the integer educational-quality score (0-5) that serves as the regression target for the classifier; the resulting 450k+ (text, score) pairs are released as fineweb-edu-llama3-annotations.
The educational quality scores (0-5) used as training labels for the classifier were generated by Llama3-70B-Instruct on 500k FineWeb samples.
Educational-value annotations used to train the FineWeb-Edu classifier were generated by Llama-3 70B Instruct (per the 'Llama3-annotated' label of the annotations dataset).
